ManagementTextImportant.html.en: IMPORTANT: Attempting to manage LP/GOLPP symptoms with medications is controversial. There is no medication that can restore movement to paralyzed laryneal tissue. That means that even with medication, the risk of breathing crises and a potentially fatal suffocation event is very real. So while medications can't prevent a breathing emergency, many veterinary professionals believe some medications can have a positive effect on symptoms and improve an LP/GOLPP patient's quality of life.
ManagementTextImportant.html._source: IMPORTANT: Attempting to manage LP/GOLPP symptoms with medications is controversial. There is no medication that can restore movement to paralyzed laryneal tissue. That means that even with medication, the risk of breathing crises and a potentially fatal suffocation event is very real. So while medications can't prevent a breathing emergency, many veterinary professionals believe some medications can have a positive effect on symptoms and improve an LP/GOLPP patient's quality of life.

ManagementTextNote.html.en: Note: Medications for arthritis/osteoarthritis pain such as NSAIDS (Rimadyl, carprofen) and NGF blockers (Librela, Beransa) may improve mobility by reducing pain, but they aren't specifically prescribed for LP/GOLPP and therefore and aren't included in the 'Medical management' section of the survey.
ManagementTextNote.html._source: Note: Medications for arthritis/osteoarthritis pain such as NSAIDS (Rimadyl, carprofen) and NGF blockers (Librela, Beransa) may improve mobility by reducing pain, but they aren't specifically prescribed for LP/GOLPP and therefore and aren't included in the 'Medical management' section of the survey.
